messages.author: marius T
messages.text: Following up on my last post about Meshcore-proxy, I have created a Meshcore client that supports the usual connection methods as well as WebSockets. https://github.com/andrewheadricke/mcui As the README outlines, it is a dumb client, it does not interact with the radios internal storage at all, it simply listens for RXDataLog messages. All identies, contacts, channels, are all stored in browser localStorage. All the mapping and link tracing is all done passively from incoming packets. No 3rd party / external websites/ apis invovled (apart from OSM tiles obviously). The app is usable but still alpha in reality. You can access a pre built version at https://mcui.canop2p.com or install the android app from https://zapstore.dev/apps/naddr1qvzqqqr7pvpzp0m4lv26wskze24h697t8m0euacr9hyj2fzgwkey2sg2ms5ljhkhqqgxxmmd9e3kzmn0wqe8qtndvd6kjn7cd92 The android version ONLY supports websockets, if you want WebBLE or WebSerial you need to use the hosted version.

No 3rd party / external websites/ apis invovled (apart from OSM tiles obviously). The app is usable but still alpha in reality. You can access a pre built version at https://mcui.canop2p.com or install the android app from https://zapstore.dev/apps/naddr1qvzqqqr7pvpzp0m4lv26wskze24h697t8m0euacr9hyj2fzgwkey2sg2ms5ljhkhqqgxxmmd9e3kzmn0wqe8qtndvd6kjn7cd92 The android version ONLY supports websockets, if you want WebBLE or WebSerial you need to use the hosted version.

GitHub - andrewheadricke/mcui: A MeshCore compatible UI
A MeshCore compatible UI. Contribute to andrewheadricke/mcui development by creating an account on GitHub.

Meshcore Chat
P2P mesh radio chat

messages.author: marius T
messages.text: Just published v0.2.3, now shows the hop count and path.hash.mode in the contact list. You can also click on the cog and "View advert path" to view the path on a map
messages.content_html: Just published v0.2.3, now shows the hop count and path.hash.mode in the contact list. You can also click on the cog and "View advert path" to view the path on a map

messages.text: yeah apologies. My domain provider deactivated the domain and I couldn't get it back. Didn't want to wait 3+ months to try recover it
